content.full_text:
RAVE MEHTA
CHAIRMAN
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
September 23,
1996
The Honorable Larry Bowler
Assembly Member, Tenth District
State Capitol
Post Office Box 942849
Sacramento, ‘California
94249-0001
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-96-255
Dear Assembly Member Bowler:
This is in response to your request for advice regarding the
provisions of the Political Reform Act
(the “Act”).
1
This advice
shall not be construed to apply to past conduct.
QUESTION
Is the use of a recreational vehicle at no charge for use as
a mobile district office considered a contribution?
CONCLUS ION
Yes.
The use of a recreational vehicle is considered a
contribution to you.
FACTS
You are a member of the California Assembly.
You were given
the use of a recreational vehicle at no charge.
You are using the
recreational vehicle as a mobile district office to give your
constituents the opportunity to meet and discuss legislative
issues that concern them.
ANALYSIS
Section 82015 defines “contribution” as any payment,
forgiveness of a loan,
a payment of a loan by a third party,
or an
1
Government Code Sections 81000-91015.
Commission regulations
appear at Title 2,
Sections 18000—18995 of the California Code of
Regulations.
428 J STREET, SUITE 800, SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814
(916) 322-5660


File No.
A—96—255
Page
2
enforceable promise to make a payment except to the extent that
full and adequate consideration is received unless it is clear
from the surrounding circumstances that it is not made for
political purposes.
A payment is made for political purposes if
it is:
(1)
for the purpose of influencing or attempting to
influence the action of the voters for or against the nomination
or election of a candidate or candidates,
or the qualification or
passage of any measure; p
(2)
received by or made at the behest
of a candidate.
(Regulation 18215(a).)
Section 82007 defines “candidate,” in pertinent part,
as any
individual who is listed on the ballot for elective office.
An
individual who becomes a candidate shall retain his or her status
as a candidate until such time as that status is terminated
pursuant to Section 84214.
Thus,
as an elected official,
you are
considered a candidate under Section 82007.
Consequently,
payments you receive are presumed to be for political purposes.
(Danner Advice Letter,
No. A-96-039.)
2
The definition of a contribution includes goods or services
you receive at no charge or at a discount from the fair market
value,
unless the discount is given in the regular course of
business to members of the public.
(Regulation 18215(b) (3).)
Accordingly,
the use of the recreational vehicle at no charge is a
contribution to you.
If you have any further questions, please feel free to
contact me at
(916)
322—5660.
Sincerely,
Steven G.
Churchwell
General Counse
,
By:
Lia e Randolph
Counsel, Legal Division
SGC:LR: ak
2
Please note that payments may also result in the making of
gifts to a public official.
(Section 82028.)
Under these facts,
we have analyzed the payments as contributions made for a
political purpose.
Therefore, it is not necessary to discuss
whether a gift would result.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Is the use of a recreational vehicle at no charge for use as
a mobile district office considered a contribution?

CONCLUSION: Yes.
The use of a recreational vehicle is considered a
contribution to you.
